hierarchy: company -> business -> income/expense -> project as typical construction carrier

families.source_of_truth: res.company plus sc.business.entity and capability/policy registries
families.target_problem: Define the company as the managed主体 and control which business capabilities are installed, visible, and approvable.
families.boundary_rule: Governance models may configure company/platform behavior, but they must not become business fact documents.

families.target_problem: Keep customer, supplier, subcontractor, and other counterparty identity reusable across income and expense business.
families.boundary_rule: Custom fields may preserve construction semantics and legacy anchors, but counterparty identity remains native.

families.target_problem: Represent the typical construction business carrier where income, expense, progress, cost, quality, safety, and treasury facts meet.
families.boundary_rule: Project is the dominant execution anchor, but company-level, pre-project, and platform facts must not be forced into project scope.

families.source_of_truth: product.template for identity and sc.material.catalog for construction catalog surface
families.target_problem: Manage materials as construction-visible resources while retaining native product identity and procurement/stock integration.
families.boundary_rule: Product identity stays native; material catalog is an industry surface and replay bridge, not a second product master.

families.source_of_truth: construction income/professional contract models and tender lifecycle models
families.target_problem: Track business the company wins or prepares to win before it becomes project execution and income realization.
families.boundary_rule: Tender is pre-project income opportunity; income contracts own commercial commitment and should not be hidden inside generic project fields.

families.target_problem: Track expense commitments for procurement, subcontract, materials, services, and general business spending.
families.boundary_rule: General/procurement contracts and project construction contracts need an explicit ownership split to avoid duplicate fact ownership.

families.source_of_truth: payment.request for intent/approval and sc.payment.execution for paid fact
families.target_problem: Separate expense payment intent, approval, and actual cash movement.
families.boundary_rule: Request owns workflow intent; execution owns the business fact that payment happened.

families.family: receipt income invoice and tax realization
families.target_problem: Record income-side receipt, invoice, and tax evidence as auditable construction facts.
families.boundary_rule: Income realization facts may link to native accounting, but the construction evidence document remains the industry source.

families.source_of_truth: sc.fund.account for account master, sc.treasury.reconciliation for reconciliation document, sc.treasury.ledger as derived ledger
families.target_problem: Manage cash accounts and reconcile income/expense movement around company and project business.
families.boundary_rule: Account master, reconciliation document, and derived ledger must remain separate responsibilities.

families.family: project budget BOQ and cost control
families.source_of_truth: construction BOQ, project budget, settlement, and cost-period documents
families.target_problem: Represent planned and actual cost structure for project-managed construction business.
families.boundary_rule: Cost documents own construction cost semantics; accounting owns posted financial transactions.

families.source_of_truth: material plan, acceptance, rental, stock, settlement, and procurement documents
families.target_problem: Control material procurement, site acceptance, consumption, stock visibility, rental, and settlement for project expense.
families.boundary_rule: Native stock/purchase owns logistics/accounting integration; industry docs own site business semantics.

families.source_of_truth: labor, equipment, and subcontract plan/request/register/settlement documents
families.target_problem: Manage construction resource usage and settlement that create project cost and payable facts.
families.boundary_rule: Professional resource lifecycles should not collapse back into one generic contract or payment table.

families.family: progress quality safety risk and diary
families.source_of_truth: progress, quality, safety, risk, site photo, and construction diary documents
families.target_problem: Capture project execution control facts beyond finance: progress, compliance, safety, quality, and site evidence.
families.boundary_rule: These models are project execution facts; they may feed reports but should not be replaced by projections.

families.family: document admin payroll and office operations
families.target_problem: Manage operational evidence and administrative workflow around company and project execution.
families.boundary_rule: Administrative evidence can support business workflows, but should not become the source for income/expense amounts unless explicitly promoted.

families.source_of_truth: deterministic refresh from native, industry, and legacy/formal facts
families.target_problem: Give management current company/project visibility without making reports writable business facts.
families.boundary_rule: Projection models must be rebuildable and must not become primary workflow write surfaces.
families.status: clear if refresh ownership stays explicit

families.source_of_truth: source-fidelity old-system facts and mapping tables
families.target_problem: Preserve historical evidence and migration traceability without defining future industry model shape.
families.boundary_rule: Legacy carriers are replay/import surfaces only; customer data repair must not set the system hierarchy.

families.family: workflow approval dictionary audit and validation
families.source_of_truth: workflow, approval, dictionary, audit, and validation models
families.target_problem: Control how business facts are approved, validated, classified, audited, and made operable.
families.boundary_rule: Workflow and policy models govern facts; they do not own the underlying income/expense/project fact.

families.family: scene capability subscription and frontend contract runtime
families.source_of_truth: scene, capability, and pack registries; subscription and entitlement authority is platform-core owned in smart_core
families.target_problem: Expose backend business capability to the generic frontend renderer through platform contracts.
families.boundary_rule: Scene contracts describe entry semantics; they must not embed customer-specific rendering or replay logic.

families.source_of_truth: native model extensions and compatibility mixins
families.native_dependency: project, account, purchase, stock, product, task, tier validation
families.target_problem: Keep native/OCA behavior compatible with construction business facts without replacing native ownership.
families.boundary_rule: Extensions may add anchors and hooks; they must not duplicate native transaction ownership.
